rules.title: Committed records are append-only
rules.lesson: C12/CF7 — never edit a committed record, write the next one; MDR + content-hash closes the delete-recreate and merge-hidden holes
rules.rationale: A record that can be silently rewritten is not a record — the forensic tier's value is that what was written stays written. Proven from history, not promised in prose. Stays warn by the M7 ruling: REC is the only candidate at applies_to:all, and a severity-by-posture mechanism has no consumer (revive: real demand for REC-02-at-admit); blocking comes from the tooling gates.
rules.fix: Don't edit records/ files after commit — supersede with a new record (new session log, new JDG, superseding ADR). If a landed record must die, that's a judgment: record a JDG and leave the tombstone.

rules.title: Landed records are scrub-clean
rules.lesson: C34/FS4 — write-time and push-time scrubs are the gates; this is the re-scan of what actually landed (blocking comes from the tooling gates (REC promotion deferred by the M7 ruling))
rules.rationale: Session logs and judgments are prose written in a hurry — the most likely home for a pasted token. One scan API (src/scrub.mjs) means the rule and the write gate can never disagree about what a secret looks like.
rules.fix: Rotate the secret, rewrite it out of history, and allowlist true false-positives with a dated judgment: baseline scrub --allow <finding-id> --allow-reason "...".

rules.title: Every record fact has one home
rules.lesson: C09/CF10 — one home per fact, declared precedence; duplication detection is a heuristic, so this rule is pinned warn-only
rules.rationale: Two files claiming the same JDG/CLM id (or the session narrative kept in two directories) means the next reader picks one at random — the drift C09 exists to prevent.
rules.fix: Keep one owned file per record id; renumber the newer duplicate (see CONTRACT.md's merge-renumbering rule) and move or delete the legacy docs/session-log/ home — left in place it stays a second home and this warn cannot clear.

rules.title: Records are covered by a push-time secret gate
rules.lesson: F7/CF6 — baseline's scrub is layered, not "mandatory"; when a dedicated scanner owns the gate, baseline delegates to it
rules.rationale: baseline log scrubs what it writes; hand-written records need a gate at the push boundary (FS4: for a public repo, push is the deadline). Evidence of gitleaks/pre-commit/a pre-push hook satisfies this; GitHub push protection also satisfies it but isn't observable at rest — M6's forge rules assert it live.
rules.fix: Wire gitleaks (CI or .pre-commit-config.yaml), or commit the scaffold hook where the checker can see it (copy the skill's hooks/scrub-pre-push.sh into your repo's hooks/) and install it per clone (cp hooks/scrub-pre-push.sh .git/hooks/pre-push — .git/ itself is invisible at rest). GitHub push protection satisfies the intent but isn't observable at rest; M6's forge rules assert it live.

check.then_fail_detail: no push-time secret gate visible at rest — wire gitleaks, or commit hooks/scrub-pre-push.sh (+ per-clone install); GitHub push protection satisfies the intent but only M6 can assert it live
